meta.title: Emergency Response Plan
meta.subtitle: Scenario-keyed ERP — {named site/facility}
meta.author: HSE Operations
meta.deid_notice: The call-out tree in any circulated copy refers to responders by role + duty-phone label. Personal names and personal mobile numbers were pseudonymised before drafting; the internal master contact list (the re-identification key) is held separately and is not part of this distributed document.

sections.text: This emergency response plan is keyed to {named site/facility} (ISO 45001 clause 8.2). Occupancy: {headcount} across {shift pattern}; the plan accounts for staff, contractors and visitors who may be on site during each scenario.

rows.control: Eliminate / reduce the ignition source & stored quantity for the flammable scenario
Permit-to-work + housekeeping controls on the scenario source

rows.title: Fire/explosion — response procedure
rows.description: Detection/alarm -> immediate actions (raise alarm, isolate, evacuate route X) -> escalation/call-out -> muster at {point} -> all-clear. Scenario-keyed, not a generic evacuate.

rows.title: Chemical/gas release — response procedure
rows.description: Detection -> upwind evacuation -> isolation point {ref} -> external-responder notification -> muster + headcount.

sections.caption: Call-out tree — every role has a named deputy; role + duty-phone labels in this circulated copy (personal contacts held in the separate internal master).

sections.text: Primary muster point: {named point, e.g. north car park}; secondary: {named}. Evacuation routes {refs}; roll-call against the access register; provision for visitors, contractors and persons requiring assistance. Capacity matched to peak occupancy.

sections.caption: Responder-integration sheet — access, isolation points, site interface.
Dated drill schedule — cadence from KB-DATA-DRILL-FREQ (quote source+year; [GAP] where unresolved). Debrief + corrective actions after each drill.

sections.text: ERP grounded in ISO 45001 clause 8.2 (and 29 CFR 1910.38 / RRFSO 2005 art.15 / Factories Act s.41B via hse-india as applicable). SME review by the Emergency Planning Officer persona completed; this is decision-support only and must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person before reliance.

For continuity of critical activities AFTER the emergency is controlled (recovery of operations, RTO / RPO / MTPD), see the business-continuity-plan skill — the ERP plans the immediate response, the BCP plans the recovery. The two are complementary and not merged.
